ddls:
CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the API service */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the service does */,
  "endpoint" TEXT /* Base endpoint of the API service */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the service */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the service was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the service was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource */,
  "resource_name" TEXT /* Name of the resource accessed in the API */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of the resource (e.g., user, post) */,
  "resource_description" TEXT /* Description of the resource */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the resource was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the resource was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "http_status_codes" (
  "status_code" INTEGER /* HTTP status code */,
  "status_message" TEXT /* Description of the status code */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the status code (e.g., Informational, Success, Redirection, Client Error, Server Error) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the logs */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log entry being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of the access event */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the logs */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user accessing the logs */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "errors" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error entry */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log entry where error occurred */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Description of the error encountered */,
  "error_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error occurred */,
  "error_stacktrace" TEXT /* Stack trace of the error (if applicable) */,
  "retry_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the API call was retried before the error */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who encountered the error (if authenticated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_codes" (
  "error_code" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error code */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Description of the error */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the error code (e.g., Authentication, Validation, System) */,
  "suggested_action" TEXT /* Suggested action to resolve the error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "process_times" (
  "process_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each process time entry */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log entry related to this process time */,
  "process_time" TEXT /* Time taken to process the API call */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the API call processing */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the API call processing */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the API call (if authenticated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("process_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_process_times_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "extra_info" (
  "info_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each info entry */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log entry related to this information */,
  "info_key" TEXT /* Key of the additional information */,
  "info_value" TEXT /* Value of the additional information */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the extra information was added */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the extra information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("info_id")
)
